name: Roy McCurdy
abstract: Roy McCurdy (born November 28, 1936) is a jazz drummer.

interviews.title: Roy McCurdy interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Cannonball Adderley Quintet, Oscar Peterson, and Mahavishnu Orchestra
Newport Jazz Festival: Cannonball Adderley Quintet, Oscar Peterson, and Mahavishnu Orchestra
JVC Jazz Festival: Mel Tormé and Nancy Wilson, Singers
JVC Jazz Festival: Jazzmobile Salute to Dr. George Butler
JVC Jazz Festival: Nancy Wilson 70th Birthday Concert
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

works.t: Shall We Dance: They Can't Take That Away From Me

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: I did one beautiful quartet date with Art Farmer called “Perception” with Roy McCurdy, Tommy Williams and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Vega
about_quotes.evidence: I recently played with Roy McCurdy, his last drummer. I did a West Coast thing with him up the Pacific Northwest.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Vega interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 20, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Roy and I obviously we ran into, I know Roy from the Cannonball Adderley days. But he’s been on the road with me for sixteen years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Shelly Berg
about_quotes.evidence: I think Roy McCurdy, who I play with a fair bit in Los Angeles, has quite a few of those things.
about_quotes.doc_title: Shelly Berg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Gap Mangione
about_quotes.evidence: But of course we were with Sal Nistico, Roy McCurdy, the band was not shy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gap Mangione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rochester, New York, January 11, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Wofford
about_quotes.evidence: Roy McCurdy who was a good friend of mine and we had played together a lot
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Wofford interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998
